methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Pre-IPO), post-money $23B

data.narrative:
Rebellions is a pre-IPO semiconductor company valued at $2.34B following $625M+ in total funding. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 12 same-sector peers spanning similar valuation tiers, comprising 8 private companies (including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor) and 3 public semiconductor names (ALAB, NVDA, AMD). The peer range spans $500M to $7.0B.

Peer-median analysis is the primary applicable method. Across the 12-company peer set, the median valuation stands at $1.8B, providing a direct benchmark for same-stage, same-sector comparables. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied; while the public semiconductor median P/S is 24.2x across the three tickers, Rebellions' revenue is not disclosed, as is typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.5B to $2.0B, with a median of $1.8B. Rebellions' current $2.34B valuation sits above this range. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data precluding margin-adjusted multiples, the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($500M–$7.0B) reflecting heterogeneous stage and technology risk, and limited insight into Rebellions' specific market traction relative to peers. The single applicable method constrains confidence; additional data on revenue or comparable transaction multiples would strengthen the analysis.
